source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the ‘Log_disconnections’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘On’

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the ‘Log_disconnections’ Database Flag for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL Instance Is Set to ‘On’
input_row.description: Enabling the log_disconnections setting logs the end of each session, including the session duration.
input_row.rationale: PostgreSQL does not log session details such as duration and session end by default. Enabling the log_disconnections setting will create log entries at the end of each session which can be useful in troubleshooting issues and determine any unusual activity across a time period. The log_disconnections and log_connections work hand in hand and generally, the pair would be enabled/disabled together. This recommendation is applicable to PostgreSQL database instances. Impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance to open its Instance Overview page 3. Go to the Configuration card. 4. Under Database flags, check the value of log_disconnections flag is configured as expected. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Ensure the below command returns on for every Cloud SQL PostgreSQL database instance: gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '.[].settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_disconnections")|.value'
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the PostgreSQL instance where the database flag needs to be enabled. 3. Click Edit. 4. Scroll down to the Flags section. 5. To set a flag that has not been set on the instance before, click Add a Database Flag, choose the flag log_disconnections from the drop-down menu and set the value as on. 6. Click Save. 7. Confirm the changes under Flags on the Overview page. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Configure the log_disconnections database flag for every Cloud SQL PosgreSQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --database-flags log_disconnections=on Note : This command will overwrite all previously set database flags. To keep those and add new ones, include the values for all flags to be set on the instance; any flag not specifically included is set to its default value. For flags that do not take a value, specify the flag name followed by an equals sign ("="). Default Value: By default log_disconnections is off. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- DISCONNECTIONS Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.
input_row.impact: Turning on logging will increase the required storage over time. Mismanaged logs may cause your storage costs to increase. Setting custom flags via command line on certain instances will cause all omitted flags to be reset to defaults. This may cause you to lose custom flags and could result in unforeseen complications or instance restarts. Because of this, it is recommended you apply these flags changes during a period of low usage.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags 2. https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/runtime-config-logging.html#GUC-LOG- DISCONNECTIONS Additional Information: WARNING: This patch modifies database flag values, which may require your instance to be restarted. Check the list of supported flags - https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/flags - to see if your instance will be restarted when this patch is submitted. Note : some database flag settings can affect instance availability or stability and remove the instance from the Cloud SQL SLA. For information about these flags, see Operational Guidelines. Note : Configuring the above flag does not require restarting the Cloud SQL instance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control in question can be fully verified using GCP APIs and commands through the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) because:

✓ The audit field provides a clear CLI command (`gcloud sql instances list`) to check the `log_disconnections` flag.
✓ The `log_disconnections` flag is a standard property of Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances and can be accessed via GCP APIs.
✓ The Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) has methods to check this configuration setting.
✓ No need for SSH access or manual human judgment; the required information is exposed via GCP commands.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_log_disconnections_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

The automated program will perform the following checks:

- Use the Google Cloud CLI command: `gcloud sql instances list --format=json` to retrieve the list of Cloud SQL instances.
- Use a command-line processing tool like `jq` to parse through the JSON output and verify that the `log_disconnections` flag is set to `on` for all PostgreSQL database instances.
- Enumerate all Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances within the project to ensure comprehensive coverage.
- Validate the configuration property `log_disconnections` is explicitly set to `on`.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - Pass: All instances with `log_disconnections` flag configured as `on`.
  - Fail: If any instance is missing this configuration or set to a value other than `on`.

This approach will ensure that all PostgreSQL instances have the logging of disconnections enabled, in alignment with the CIS Benchmark requirements.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be fully assessed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). No manual review or SSH access is required.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:**  
  The `gcloud sql instances describe` and `gcloud sql instances list` commands provide access to instance flags, including `log_disconnections`, for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instances.
- **GCP CLI/Config:**  
  The approach leverages standard GCP CLI commands and does not require GCP Config or CloudFormation (which is AWS-specific).

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Correctness of Automation Details:**  
  - The use of `gcloud sql instances list --format=json` is valid for enumerating Cloud SQL instances.
  - However, the `log_disconnections` flag is not directly visible in the output of `gcloud sql instances list`. Instead, you must use `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` to see the database flags for each instance.
  - The automation should iterate through each instance and inspect the `settings.databaseFlags` array for the `log_disconnections` flag.
- **Program Name Convention:**  
  - The program name `gcp_sql_instance_log_disconnections_enabled` is clear and descriptive, but could be slightly improved for specificity (see Improvements).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The assessment does not specify filtering for PostgreSQL instances only (Cloud SQL also supports MySQL and SQL Server). The check should ensure it only evaluates PostgreSQL instances.
  - The check should also handle cases where the flag is not set at all (default is `off`).
- **CLI Command Accuracy:**  
  - Should use:
    ```
    gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name,databaseVersion)"
    ```
    to get instance names and types, then:
    ```
    gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format="json(settings.databaseFlags)"
    ```
    to check the flag.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Filter for PostgreSQL Instances:**  
  Only check instances where `databaseVersion` starts with `POSTGRES`.
- **Correct Flag Retrieval:**  
  Use `gcloud sql instances describe` per instance to retrieve `settings.databaseFlags`.
- **Handle Missing Flags:**  
  If `log_disconnections` is absent, treat as non-compliant (default is `off`).
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  For clarity and convention, consider:  
  `gcp_sql_postgresql_instance_log_disconnections_enabled`
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  The same can be achieved via the [Cloud SQL Admin API](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/admin-api/rest/v1beta4/instances/get) (`instances.get`), parsing the `settings.databaseFlags` field.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**  
  Explicitly state that any PostgreSQL instance missing the flag or set to a value other than `on` is a fail.
- **Sample Pseudocode:**
    ```bash
    for i in $(gcloud sql instances list --format="value(name,databaseVersion)" | grep POSTGRES | awk '{print $1}'); do
      gcloud sql instances describe $i --format="json(settings.databaseFlags)" | jq '.settings.databaseFlags[] | select(.name=="log_disconnections") | .value'
    done
    ```

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The control is well understood, and the technical path to automate it is clear and supported by GCP APIs and CLI.
- Minor technical corrections are needed, but the overall approach is sound.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Modify** the automation details to:
  - Use `gcloud sql instances describe` per instance.
  - Filter for PostgreSQL instances only.
  - Ensure the check handles missing flags as non-compliant.
  - Update the program name for clarity and convention.
- **Example Updated Automation Steps:**
  1. List all Cloud SQL instances and filter for PostgreSQL.
  2. For each PostgreSQL instance, use `gcloud sql instances describe` to retrieve `settings.databaseFlags`.
  3. Check if `log_disconnections` is present and set to `on`.
  4. Fail if any instance is missing the flag or set to a value other than `on`.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section             | Assessment             |
|---------------------|-----------------------|
| Approach Validation | AGREE                  |
| Technical Accuracy  | 8/10                   |
| Improvements        | See above              |
| Confidence          | HIGH                   |
| Recommendation      | MODIFY (see above)     |

---
